question: Did the model output the magnitude of the velocity at point C (v_c) as v_c = E/B?
Did the model output the work done against friction (W_f) as W_f = mgh - mE²/(2B²) (or an algebraically equivalent expression)?
Did the model output the final velocity at point P (v_p) as v_p = √{v_D² + [(qE/m)² + g²]t²}?
Does the final answer for v_p include only the given quantities (v_D, q, E, m, g, t) and are the units consistent?
Did the model correctly identify that the slider starts from rest and the normal force N decreases as velocity increases due to the Lorentz force?
Did the model correctly apply the Left-Hand Rule to determine that the Lorentz force points horizontally to the left (opposing the electric force) during the downward motion?
Did the model correctly identify that the condition for the slider leaving the vertical plane MN (point C) is when the normal force N becomes zero?
Did the model correctly set up the horizontal force balance equation qE = qvB + N (or qE = qv_cB at the moment of detachment)?

Did the model correctly apply the Work-Energy Theorem for the motion from A to C, accounting for positive work by gravity, negative work by friction, and zero work by the Lorentz and Electric forces (due to perpendicularity/constraint)?
Did the model correctly identify that at point D (maximum velocity), the velocity vector is perpendicular to the resultant of the electric force and gravity?
Did the model correctly recognize that after point D (with B removed), the slider moves under the resultant of two constant forces: gravity (mg) and electric force (qE)?
Did the model correctly calculate the effective acceleration g' = √[(qE/m)² + g²] for the motion from D to P?
Did the model correctly derive the final velocity v_p using the vector relationship v_p² = v_D² + (g't)² based on the perpendicularity of the initial velocity v_D and the acceleration?
If a force diagram for the motion along MN is generated, is the Electric force (qE) drawn pointing to the right and the Lorentz force drawn pointing to the left?
If a force diagram for the motion along MN is generated, is gravity (mg) drawn pointing downward and friction drawn pointing upward?
If the magnetic field is depicted in an image, does the diagram use appropriate notation (e.g., dots or ⊙ symbols) to represent the field pointing outwards?
Does the generated trajectory diagram depict a vertical path from A to C and a curved path separating from the wall after C?
If a diagram for point D is generated, does it visually reflect the condition that the velocity vector v_D is perpendicular to the resultant force (combined qE and mg)?
Are the generated images clear, with distinct arrows for force vectors and legible labels for points (A, C, D, P)?
Do the coordinate system orientations and point labels (A, C, D, P) remain consistent across all generated images?
Do the directions of forces in the generated diagrams align with the text description (e.g., E-field right, Lorentz left, Friction up)?
Does the depicted trajectory in the images align with the text's description of the motion phases (vertical sliding followed by curvilinear motion)?
If the text describes the velocity composition at P using perpendicular vectors, does the image visually support this geometric relationship?
